description: Analytic summary for the no-go, decoupled-block, and echo claims.

generic_many_block_claim.generic_meaning: The exact cancellation set is a measure-zero subset of durations and amplitudes where the kernel matrix has a special null relation or some addressed amplitudes vanish.
generic_many_block_claim.statement: For more than two blocks, the spectator-induced Z coefficients form a quadratic system zeta_n = -sum_{m != n} lambda_m^2 K(Delta_nm,T). These constraints are independent of azimuth and are not satisfied on an open set of nontrivial target rotations.

load_instructions: Load `analytic_summary.json` as JSON and inspect the documented result rows and metadata.

two_block_no_go.kernel_definition: K(Delta,T) = 1/Delta - sin(Delta T)/(Delta^2 T) for the square-envelope study pulse.
two_block_no_go.statement: For two simultaneously addressed blocks with nonzero amplitudes lambda_0 and lambda_1, the second-order spectator-induced Z coefficients are proportional to -lambda_1^2 K(Delta,T) and +lambda_0^2 K(Delta,T). For generic K(Delta,T) != 0, both cannot vanish unless one drive amplitude is zero.
